title: Advance ISO/PWI 13584-26 ed2 to stage 20
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 4, 2026 -- May 15, 2026

actions.message:
SC4 resolves to advance ISO/PWI 13584-26 ed2 to stage 20 with the
following terms of reference:

* Development Stage 20 (NP) with a standard development track (SDT) of 24
months,

* Target dates:

** Circulation of 1st Working Draft: 2026-10-09
** Committee Draft consultation: 2026-12-12
** DIS submission: 2027-05-31
** Publication: 2027-12-11

* ISO 13584-26 supports the UN Sustainable Development Goal 9 (Industry,
Innovation and Infrastructure) by ensuring efficient use of data in industrial settings
in concert with the larger part of the ISO 13584 series.

* ISO 13584-26 supports the UN Sustainable Development Goal 12 (Responsible
Consumption and Production) by properly identifying data, its meaning and
ensuring the responsible use of that data in industrial settings.

* Allocates the work to JWG 24 with Mr. Hiroshi Murayama as Project Leader,

* Nominate the following experts:

** AFNOR: Mr. Christophe Mouton, Mr. Yves Keraron,
** ANSI: Mr. Ronald Tse,
** DIN: Mr. Gernot Rossi,
** JISC: Mr. Yoshiaki Sonoda
** KATS: Prof. Soonhung Han
** SABS: Dr. Salomon De Jager

* Provide OSD access using the existing document to enable JWG 24 to prepare
the Working Draft, and

* Requests member bodies nominate experts to the project’s work.

considerations.message: Noting the work of JWG 24 in the refinement of ISO/PWI 13584-26 ed2 by the project team

snippet: SC4 resolves to advance ISO/PWI 13584-26 ed2 to stage 20 with the following terms of reference: * Development Stage 20 (NP) with a standard development track (SDT) of 24 months, * Target dates: ** ...

title: Launch ISO/NP 8000-60 ed2 revision as new project
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 4, 2026 -- May 15, 2026

actions.message:
SC4 requests its Committee Manager to Register with ISO/CS ISO/AWI TS
8000-60 edition 2 with the following terms of reference:

* Incorporate the new scope to include parts 65 and 66 from the ISO 8000 series,
* Development stage 20 with a standard development track (SDT) of 36 months,

* Target dates:
** Circulation of 1st Working Draft: 2026-12-14
** Committee Draft consultation: 2027-03-01
** DIS submission: 2027-12-13
** Publication: 2029-05-31

* Allocates the work to WG 13 with Christophe Mouton as Project Leader, Prof.
Changsoo Lee as Project Co-Leader, and Ms Hayley Thompson as Project Editor

* ISO/TS 8000-60 supports the UN Sustainable Development Goal 9 (Industry,
Innovation and Infrastructure) by providing concepts for implementing, assessing
and improving data quality management in various industrial sectors.

* Register the following list of NSBs with their nominated representatives as active
participants in the revision:

** USA: Mr. Peter Benson
** Norway: Mr. Tor Arne Irgens,
** Korea: Mr. Sungjoon LIM
** South Africa: Dr. Salomon De Jager

* Provide OSD access to enable WG 13 to prepare the Working Draft using the
previous edition as a basis.

considerations.message: Noting the ballot results of the Systematic Review for ISO/SRR TS 8000-60 taken on 2025-12-02 and the recommendation by WG 13

title: Launch ISO/NP 10303-15 revision as a new project
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 4, 2026 -- May 15, 2026

actions.message:
SC4 requests its Committee Manager to coordinate with ISO/CS and initiate
the revision of ISO 10303-15, as a New Project on SDT 18 track with the
following terms of reference:

Proposed date for first meeting: 2026-05-21

Dates for key milestones:

* Circulation of 1st Working Draft (if any) to experts: 2026-05-07
* Committee Draft Consultation (if any): 2026-06-15
* DTS submission: 2026-09-15
* Publication: 2027-01-15

Identifies SDG Goal 9 with the following rationale:

* The ISO 10303 standard in total is identified against Goal 9 and this
part in included in that series, and

* To enable the interoperability between industry and IT architectures in
industrial data applications

SC4 allocates the project to WG 11 and appoints Mr. Kevin Le Tutour as
Project Leader with nominations of the following experts to participate
and welcome additional experts to participate in the project:

* AFNOR: Mr. Frédéric Darré, Mr. Pierre Duchier, Mr. Erwan Livolant, Mr.
Kevin Le Tutour
* DIN: Mr. Guillaume Hirel, Mr. Max Ungerer
* BSI: Mr. Phil Spiby, Mr. Robert Swindells
* ANSI: Mr. Patrick Walsh, Mr. Stuart Galt, Mr. Tom Thurman
* SIS: Mr. Mikael Hedlind

Utilize the ISO OSD with a document provided by WG 11 that will replace
the edition currently in publication.

considerations.message:

incorporate capability enhancements from industry requirements identified
in ISO JIRA TCSC410303-2756

title: Confirm retention of ISO/TS 10303-17:2022 after systematic review
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 4, 2026 -- May 15, 2026

actions.message:
SC4 notes that the Systematic Review ballot for ISO/TS 10303-17:2022
indicated that a simple majority of the P-members of the committee propose
confirmation.  Furthermore, the results of the ballot indicates
adoption/use in Denmark, Japan, Finland and Norway, which together with
ongoing ISO 10303-242 development activity in France and Germany
demonstrate that the document is used internationally and has been adopted
with or without change or is used in at least five countries.

SC4 concludes that this document meets the criteria for retention after
systematic review and shall be made available in its existing form

SC4 requests its Committee Manager disposition the SRR results accordingly
and notify ISO/CS of the Committee’s decision.

title: Appointment of liaison representatives to ISO/TC 154
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 4, 2026 -- May 15, 2026

actions.message:
SC 4 requests its Committee Manager to extend the liaison from ISO/TC
184/SC 4 to ISO/TC 154, and appoints Mr. Christophe Mouton, Mr. Peter
Benson, Ms Hayley Thompson, Mr. Yoshiaki Sonoda, Mr. Yves Keraron, and Mr.
Zhang Wei Qun, (Kevin) as new liaison representatives for a three-year
term [2026-2029], with appreciation for their willingness to serve in this
capacity.

considerations.message:
Recognizing:

* that ISO/TC 154 is developing ISO/NP 26087 Processes and data in
carbon market monitoring reporting verification (MRV) -- Data quality
assessment, which introduces structured methods for evaluating
completeness, timeliness, traceability, accuracy, and comparability of
MRV data, as described in ISO/TC 154 N 1641 and N 1642;

* that these concepts align closely with the data quality principles,
vocabularies, and industrial data frameworks maintained by ISO/TC 184/SC
4;

* that WG 13 has expressed strong interest in contributing expertise to
ensure consistency and interoperability between SC 4 data quality
standards and the MRV data quality methodologies under development;

title: Establish Study Group 1 on Industrial data and digital twin
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Little Rock, United States, May 18, 2025 -- May 23, 2025

actions.message:
SC 4 establishes Study Group 1 -- Industrial data and digital twin with the following terms of reference:

* Examine the current implementations of digital twin in SC4 standards and related organizations inside and outside ISO, notably ISO/TC 184, ISO/TC 184/SC 5, IEC/TC 65,
* Develop pros and cons to each proposal put forward from JWG 16 and any developed internal to the study group,
* Recommend one of the proposals to SC4 with a draft implementation plan including timing,
* Report to SC4 at each plenary meeting the status of the study group until the group is disbanded, and
* Provide its final report to SC4 on or before June 2026.

SC4 names Kenneth Swope, Convenor, Soonhung Han, Deputy Convenor and Martin Hardwick, Deputy Convenor, with appreciation to serve in that capacity, recognizes the following experts:

* Hiroshi Murayama,
* Hyman Duan,
* Sangkeun Yoo,
* Gordon Shao,
* Bengt Olsson,
* Paul van Exel,
* Mario Tucci,
* Benjamin Martinez,
* Richard Merkel,
* Anne-Francoise Cutting-Decelle, and
* Christophe Mouton,

who have volunteered to participate and invites member bodies to nominate experts to participate in the group’s work.

considerations.message: Noting the proposal from JWG 16 regarding the situation ISO/TC 184/SC 4 finds with the current implementation of digital twin as a technical concept into the scope of industrial data, and the existing activities of ISO/TC 184/AG 2 and IEC/ISO JTC 1/SC 41 -- Internet of Things and Digital Twin

title: Launch ISO/NP Ontology-based interoperability and create WG 26
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Saint-Denis, France, June 11, 2023 -- June 17, 2023

actions.message:
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 requests its Committee Manager launch ISO/NP Automation systems and
integration -- Ontology-based interoperability with an eight-week ballot duration.

ISO/TC 184/SC 4 creates a Work Group entitled WG 26 "Ontology-based interoperability" to:

* Develop and maintain ISO xxxxx: "Automation systems and integration -
  Ontology-based interoperability"
* Develop and maintain guidance for the implementation and use of the standard series
* Develop and maintain detailed specifications for integration and re-use of
  existing reference data libraries and ontologies using the standard series

ISO/TC 184/SC 4 requests WG 26 to work in close cooperation with WG 3, WG 12, WG 15, WG
21, and WG 22 to align with, reuse and complement their existing standards, and
avoid overlaps and conflicts with ISO 10303, ISO 15926, and ISO 23247 and other SC 4 standards.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 nominates Pål Rylandsholm as convener of the work group.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 invites member bodies to nominate additional members to the work group.

subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Saint-Denis, France, June 11, 2023 -- June 17, 2023

actions.message:
SC 4 requests its Committee Manager establish a Task Force on the SC 4 Common Change
Process with the following terms of reference:

* Create a Committee Specific Procedure for WG 21, WG 22, WG 23, and JWG 24
  that harmonizes the process for maintaining standards as a database and propose
  the procedure to SC 4 for consideration as a standing document
* Revise the existing procedures for WG 21 (SC4N2617, SC4N2591), WG 22
  (SC4N2984), WG 23 (SC4N2612), and JWG 24 (SC4N3708) to incorporate the
  proposed Common Change Process and propose to SC 4 for consideration as a
  standing document
* Draft/revise user level documents for implementing the Common Change Process
  at the technology application level for each WG
* Update SC4 at each meeting on progress until expected completion in Fall 2024

SC 4 nominates the following to the Task Force with appreciation for serving on
this important task:

* Nils Sandsmark
* Jochen Haenisch
* Paul van Exel
* Dr. Tim King (Convenor)
* Hiroshi Murayama
SC 4 invites National Bodies to nominate members to the task force.

Noting that ISO 10303 SMRL is a body of work collectively managed as a set of Technical
Specification and subject to the procedures noted in SC4N2591 for managing
standards in database format, ISO 10303-1819 is managed in SC 4 as a required

having found no root cause for the discrepancy

title: Retire Bugzilla and migrate to JIRA for issue management
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, November 8, 2021 -- November 12, 2021

actions.message:
SC 4 supports the recommendation of WG 12 and agrees to retire Bugzilla as the system
of record for CR management and authorizes WG12 to start working in JIRA per the following
schedule:

|===
|Action |Date

|Stop using Bugzilla on |December 15, 2021
|Archive Bugzilla to ISO WG12 Member Area by |December 20, 2021
|Migrate Bugzilla projects to JIRA by |December 26, 2021
|Start using JIRA on |January 1, 2022
|===

SC 4 concurs with the recommendation of the Living Lab to disposition the Bugzilla
projects as follows:

|===
|Topic |Description |Bug Count |Proposed Action

|10303 example files |Example files used by implementors to understand the standard. |0 |Recommend ARCHIVAL
|10303 Recommended Practices |Includes new materials as well as old AP210 materials. |114 |Recommend ARCHIVAL
|AP210 IS1 |ISO 10303-210:2001 |1105 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-10303
|Express Engine |EXPRESS based purpose built software. |83 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-Utilities
|ISO 10303 |all issues on ISO 10303 standards |6644 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-10303
|ISO 13584 |Parts library |16 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-13584
|ISO 14306 |Open JT |327 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-14306
|ISO 29002 |Exchange of characteristic data |0 |Recommend ARCHIVAL
|SC4 Documents |SC4 Documents that are not standing documents |2 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-StandingDocs
|SC4 Standing documents |SC4 Standing documents |51 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-StandingDocs
|STEPlib environment and utilities |same |3 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-Utilities
|stepmod environment and utilities |stepmod environment and utilities |548 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-Utilities
|Technical Reports |Technical reports authored by SC4 |0 |Recommend ARCHIVAL
|test |A dummy product used to test permissions |1 |Recommend ARCHIVAL
|WG12_policy |Record of WG12 policy items. |5 |Recommend MIGRATION: TC184SC4-StandingDocs
|===

WG 12 recommends that SC 4 PPC take the action to revise the SC 4 Handbook at its
next revision noting the new system of record.
SC 4 thanks PDES, Inc. for providing outstanding custodianship of the Bugzilla environment.

considerations.message:
Noting the progress made in developing an agile environment for SC 4 CR management
and the detailed plan for cutover to the new environment

title: Update SC 4 Handbook standing documents via CIB
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 10, 2021 -- May 14, 2021

actions.message:
SC 4 requests the Committee Manager launch an eight week CIB to modify the
SC 4 Handbook to account for the following changes in approved standing
documents:

* Deprecate SC4 N2412 "Supplementary directives -- Rules for the structure
  and drafting of SC 4 standards for industrial data"
* Add SC 4 N3567 "SC 4 Supplementary directives -- Rules for the structure
  and drafting of SC 4 standards for industrial data"
* Deprecate SC4 N3422 "STEP extended architecture high-level description"
* Add SC4 N3501 "STEP extended architecture high-level description"
* Deprecate SC4 N3433 "STEP extended architecture high-level requirements"
* Add SC4 N3502 "STEP extended architecture high-level requirements"
* Deprecate SC4 N3424 "STEP extended architecture detailed STEPlib
  specifications"
* Add SC4 N3503 "STEP extended architecture detailed STEPlib specifications"
* Deprecate SC4 N3425 "STEP extended architecture publication design"
* Add SC4 N3504 "STEP extended architecture publication design"

Note: The Handbook has not been updated to account for recent deviations to
ISO Operations as a result of COVID-19. Subsequent to TMB and ISO guidance,
procedures for managing ISO TCs and SCs are covered by continuing
resolutions from the TMB and supersede guidance provided by this Handbook
on specific points covered.

considerations.message:
Noting the procedures for ISO/TC 184/SC 4 and recent approvals of SC 4
relative to standing documents

title: Establish Ad Hoc Group on UUID management for industrial data
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 10, 2021 -- May 14, 2021

actions.message:
SC 4 establishes an Ad Hoc Group "UUID management for industrial data" with
the following terms of reference:

* Define the opportunity space for UUID in context of SC 4 standards
* Identify use cases for UUID in industrial data
* Provide recommendations to SC 4 on the scope and applicability of UUIDs to
  SC 4 standards
* Provide a report to SC 4 upon completion

SC 4 nominates Martin Hardwick as Convenor with appreciation to serve in
that capacity, appoints the following experts to the group:

* Allison Barnard Feeney
* Peter Benson
* Keith Hunten
* Alan Johnston
* David Odendahl
* Thomas Thurman
* Jean Brange
* Mikael Hedlind
* Bengt Olsson
* Max Ungerer
* Hiroshi Murayama
* Hyunjeong Lee
* Nils Sandsmark

and invites National Bodies and Liaisons to nominate experts.

considerations.message:
Noting the technical discussions among experts from WG 3, WG 12, WG 15 and
others on the opportunity to align concepts on the use of UUID in industrial
data

snippet: SC 4 establishes an Ad Hoc Group "UUID management for industrial data" with the following terms of reference: * Define the opportunity space for UUID in context of SC 4 standards * Identify use cas...

title: Launch ISO 15926-4 ed.3 on 24-month track
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 3, 2020 -- May 8, 2020

actions.message:
SC 4 instructs its Committee Manager to launch edition 3 using the SDT 24
track and names Onno Paap as Project Leader and allocates the work to WG 3
with the following terms of reference:

* Update ISO 15926-4 ed.3 as presented to the Change Board during the 79th
Plenary of SC 4 on 05 May 2020

* Update the electronic inserts to ISO/NP 15926-4 ed.3 per SC4N2591,
SC4N2984, and SC4N2986 standing documents,

* Request national bodies to nominate their representatives and deputies to
the Validation Team (VT) as noted in SC4N2541, and

* To keep a register of the VT members for the practice of sending out
notifications to vote and other administrative items.

considerations.message:
Noting that new updates to ISO 15926-4 is needed and there is no change in
scope to the standard, the objective of the resolution is to deliver
ISO 15926-4 ed.3 building on the experience as gathered in the POSC Caesar
Association (PCA) data base with improvements on the following points:

1. Consistent naming and definitions
2. Reference to other RDIs in text definition
3. Reference to source
4. Improve specialization and classification according results of the
TopClass structure task force.
5. Rules for use of entity types inanimate_physical_object and
functional_object

The ISO 15926-4 ed.3 document will reflect the changes made and will be in
line with the electronic inserts update of ISO 15926-4e2. It will contain a
time plan in line with the incremental steps that will be taken.

snippet: SC 4 instructs its Committee Manager to launch edition 3 using the SDT 24 track and names Onno Paap as Project Leader and allocates the work to WG 3 with the following terms of reference: * Update ...

title: Create Task Force for SC 4 Reference Model for Industrial Data
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Virtual, May 3, 2020 -- May 8, 2020

actions.message:
SC 4 creates a Task Force titled "SC 4 Reference Model for Industrial Data"
with the following term of reference to define and publish a reference model
for SC 4 that builds upon the work of IDIOM, STEP extended architecture and
the on-going activities on ontologies:

1. Build upon the work of IDIOM, STEP extended architecture and the on-going
activities on ontologies, such as ISO 15926, ISO 13584, ISO 22745,
ISO 29002 and others;

2. Coordinate with other affected groups in SC4 on plan elements;

3. Provide periodic status reports to SC 4/PPC;

4. Deliver a reference model using model based computer interpretable
technologies;

5. Publish human readable guide (technical report) to the use of the
reference model.

The task force is requested to liaise with the "Core terms" work being
carried out by AHG 1 and with the work on an ontology for geometry and
topology being carried out by WG 12/T 1.

SC 4 nominates Jean Brangé and David Leal to lead the Task Force and appoints
the following experts to the Task Force:

* Nils Sandsmark
* Tor Arne Irgens
* Allison Barnard Feeney
* Paul van Exel
* Mario Tucci
* Ben Urick
* Ronald Tse
* Tom Thurman
* Yves Keraron

In addition, SC 4 requests nominations from member bodies and relevant
liaisons to participate in the Task Force.

snippet: SC 4 creates a Task Force titled "SC 4 Reference Model for Industrial Data" with the following term of reference to define and publish a reference model for SC 4 that builds upon the work of IDIOM,...

title: Agree on path forward for Edition 2 of ISO 10303-242
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Chicago, United States, November 4, 2018 -- November 9, 2018

actions.message:
SC 4 agrees that the following points specified by WG 12 for the way forward with AP 242ed2 DIS and the extended architecture specification shall be used as a basis for the second AP 242ed2 DIS ballot:

1. Agreement to retain ISO/TS 10303-4442 in the standard
2. Agreement to continue to provide an EXPRESS representation for the Integrated Resources, AIM, ARM and Domain Model
3. Agreement to retain EXPRESS
4. Agreement to use SysML as the modelling language for AP 242ed2 domain model in ISO/TS 10303-4442
5. Agreement to re-ballot the standing documents N3210, N3211, and N3212 with a revision to focus the documents only on the specific materials required to support AP 242ed2 and ISO/TS 10303-4442. All materials that speak to the future state of the extended architecture will be moved to a technical report and addressed by an ad hoc group on the strategy for extended architecture with Allison Barnard Feeney as chief editor

SC 4 invites members from each NSB to join and contribute to the refinement of the content.

6. Agreement to create SMRL V8 with the required updates that support AP 242ed2.
7. Agreement to create one or more NPs that address the implementation of SysML as a modelling solution into the internal procedures of SC 4. This work will primarily impact WG 11 and coordination has taken place at the Chicago meeting to define the work required by WG 11.
8. Acknowledgement that the strategy for extended architecture contained in the technical report will require further action by the ad hoc group as there exists a strong dependency between the strategy and the implementation of follow on capabilities for AP 239ed3 and AP 243.
9. Acknowledgement that future releases of extended architecture capabilities will require update and revision of the standing documents noted. These standing documents shall be balloted incrementally as changes are declared.
10. Acknowledgement that the current priority is to resolve documented comments against the ballot content for AP 242ed2. Any discovery or comments not formally declared will be noted as a bug in the bug tracking system against a future edition.

subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Jeju, Korea, November 5, 2017 -- November 10, 2017

actions.message:
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to create WG 16 with the following title and scope
Title: Formats for Visualization and other derived forms of product data
Scope:

* Develop and maintain format and interface standards for 3D visualization of
product models, including visualization of different classes of derived information
such as geometry, product structure and others.
* Develop and maintain standards for consumption of data derived from and
associated with product models.
* Develop and maintain standards for interfaces from SC 4 product definition
standards.
* Establish liaison with other standards activities working on information models for
3D visualization
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to assign any NP within scope to WG 16

SC 4 appoints
Soonhung Han as Convenor and Christophe Mouton as Deputy Convenor each for a
three year term, with appreciation for this offer to serve in that capacity.

title: Support DPPC Task Force Recommendations
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Sandvika, Norway, May 7, 2017 -- May 12, 2017

actions.message:
SC 4 offers to support the further development of the requirements for the future DPPC
as well as the proposed JWG to maintain the DPPC.

considerations.message: Noting the report of the ISO/IEC DPPC Task Force

Recognizing many potential use cases for consuming the content of the proposed
future DPPC across multiple industry sectors, which will require various digital forms for
extracting the content, including but not limited, to tables of values, OWL and CAD
models

Considering that it is vitally important to provide tools to facilitate the conversion of
all product characteristics in current standards into digital form for incorporation into the
proposed DPPC

Noting the clear requirement to link the product properties to its existing product
definition models, including shape representation, functionality and engineering
properties

snippet: SC 4 offers to support the further development of the requirements for the future DPPC as well as the proposed JWG to maintain the DPPC.

title: Inform ISO CS about Integrated Model for Digital Manufacturing project
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Vico Equense, Italy, April 19, 2015 -- April 24, 2015

actions.message:
SC4 requests its Secretariat to inform ISO CS about the proposed Integrated Model for
Digital Manufacturing project as an example of the work that should be addressed by
the meeting suggested by ISO/TC 184 in its resolution 573.

considerations.message:
Noting that a proposal is being developed for a new edition of AP238 which will include a series of
bug fixes, incorporate new GD&T capabilities, improve integration with ISO 13399 for
cutting tools, and ISO 14649 for NC machines, and support for additive manufacture.
Other capabilities such as robotic machining may also need to be addressed. The
responsibility for these standards is currently spread across TC 29/TC 39/TC 261/TC 184.
There is therefore an opportunity to address this project through the coordination
meeting proposed by ISO/TC 184 in its recent plenary meeting, as an example of the
necessity for coordination.

title: Support future scope and interoperability efforts of JWG 12
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Belfort, France, November 2, 2014 -- November 7, 2014

actions.message:
SC 4 welcomes the progress made to date by JWG 12 and their intention to turn ISO
16739 into a useful and widely adopted set of building information management
standards.

SC 4 encourages all members of JWG 12 to strive to maintain and where necessary
even improve the interoperability of ISO 16739 and ISO 10303.

Especially in light of the envisaged development of model view definitions for ISO
16739, SC 4 requests JWG 12 to leverage the experiences of SC 4 in producing and
maintaining their application protocols over the last two decades.

SC 4 asks JWG 12 to report on their efforts to agree on a harmonized methodology for
the presentation and implementation of ISO 16739 and ISO 10303 at the next SC 4
plenary meeting in Vico Equense in April 2015.

considerations.message:
SC 4 notes the recommendations made by ISO/TC 59/SC 13/JWG 12 at their third
meeting and the related resolutions 159 and 160 approved by ISO/TC 59/SC 13 at
their plenary meeting in Toronto.

snippet: SC 4 welcomes the progress made to date by JWG 12 and their intention to turn ISO 16739 into a useful and widely adopted set of building information management standards.

title: Transfer ISO 16739 to ISO/TC 59/SC 13 administration
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Gyeongju, Korea, November 3, 2013 -- November 8, 2013

actions.message:
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 agrees to the request of ISO/TC 59/SC 13 to transfer the Standard
ISO 16739 to the administration of SC 13.

SC 4 requests its secretary to arrange any necessary transfer of the standard to the
SC 13 work program.

SC 4 requests SC 13 to ensure that SC 4 Secretariat is informed of all ballot actions in
order to circulate the documents to the members of SC 4.
SC 4 encourages SC 13 to work with SC 4 on other data-related standards.

SC 4 is prepared to nominate representatives to the proposed JWG 12 maintenance
and validation teams.

SC 4 welcomes the proposal of SC 13 to alternate co-located JWG 12 meetings
between the two parent committees.

SC 4 encourages its members to nominate appropriate additional technical experts
to participate in the work of JWG 12.

NOTE:
The necessary HTML-documentation Guidelines requested by JWG 12 are publicly
available on the SC 4 eCommittees site.

snippet: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 agrees to the request of ISO/TC 59/SC 13 to transfer the Standard ISO 16739 to the administration of SC 13.

participating in the Maintenance Team are registered as observers.

  information described by EXPRESS schemas and methodologies for

* Develop and maintain International Standards and guidance for conveying

* Develop and maintain International Standards for the implementation of

actions.message:

Title: Product characteristics and libraries

Scope:

* Develop and maintain International Standards for defining and
  exchanging product characteristics, based on open dictionaries
* Develop and maintain International Standards for defining and
  exchanging product libraries based on product characteristics
* Develop guidance for the implementation and use of the standards
* Establish liaisons with other standards groups that require services for
  the representation of product libraries and characteristics

The scope excludes reference data libraries.

SC 4 transfers work on ISO 22745 and 29002 to WG 2, and invites WG 2 to
consider the establishment of teams focused on each standard.

of WG 2.

* Develop and maintain ISO 10303 (STEP) application protocols (APs)
* Develop and maintain the information models in the STEP Module and
  Resource Library (SMRL) to support the APs, together with other common
  resources for SC 4
* Develop and maintain guidance for the use of STEP APs
* Develop and maintain standards for 3D visualization of product models
* Establish liaison with other standards activities working on information
  models useful as common resources for SC 4

SC 4 transfers the following teams and their work from WG 3 to WG 12:

* T1 Shape Representation
* T4 Materials
* T8 Product Life Cycle
* T9 Engineering Analysis
* T10 Electrical/Electronics
* T19 Mechanical Products
* T22 Building and Construction
* T24 Manufacturing

Existing WG 12 teams such as Parametrics remain unchanged.

maintain the SMRL.

maintain reference data.

SC 4 asks member bodies to nominate additional experts for the new scope
of WG 12.

title: Approve part titles for the ISO 8000 data quality series
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Suzhou, China, October 16, 2011 -- October 21, 2011

actions.message:
SC 4 modifies resolution 821 by approving the publishing of ISO 8000-150
as a Technical Specification rather than transforming that part into
ISO 8000-50 before publication.

SC4 approves the following titles for parts under the ISO 8000
Information and Data Quality project, each one being within the scope of
the approved work item:

* Data quality -- Part 8: Information and data quality: Concepts and measuring
* Data quality -- Part 9: Information and data quality: Relationship to other standards
* Data quality -- Part 10: Exchange of data: Syntax, semantic encoding, and conformance to data specification
* Data quality -- Part 20: Exchange of data: Provenance
* Data quality -- Part 30: Exchange of data: Accuracy
* Data quality -- Part 40: Exchange of data: Completeness
* Data quality -- Part 50: Quality management framework

SC4 also approves the following title for ISO 8000-311:

* Data quality -- Part 311: Usage guide for ISO 10303-59 (PDQ-S)

SC4 instructs the SC4 Secretariat to ensure that ISO receives
notification of the above information for these parts and also the
information for ISO 8000-3 ("Data quality -- Part 3: Taxonomy"), ensuring
that the catalogue on the ISO.ORG website is complete with respect to the
current WG 13 work programme.

snippet: SC 4 modifies resolution 821 by approving the publishing of ISO 8000-150 as a Technical Specification rather than transforming that part into ISO 8000-50 before publication.

title: Advance ISO 8000-1 to IS ballot and develop taxonomy of industrial data
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Bethlehem, United States, June 6, 2010 -- June 11, 2010

actions.message: SC 4 instructs its Secretariat to submit the revised version of ISO 8000-1 “Data Quality - Overview, principles and general requirements”, excluding the taxonomy, to ISO/CS for TS publication by 2010-09-15.
SC 4 approves the submission of ISO/TS 8000-1 for ballot as an International Standard.
SC 4 directs the project team to provide the Secretariat with a draft for DIS balloting as soon as possible, and directs the Secretariat to submit this part to ISO/CS for DIS ballot.

SC 4 directs WG 13 to develop a taxonomy of industrial data to include contents that correspond to the scope of SC 4 and resolves that this taxonomy forms the contents of ISO 8000-3, with the title "Data quality -- Taxonomy of industrial data" and to include a reference to the new Part 3 in the published TS for Part 1.

SC 4 requests its Secretariat to inform all other Sub-Committees of TC 184 that WG 13 is developing taxonomy of industrial data, inviting those Sub-Committees to nominate experts who are willing to contribute to the development and review of the taxonomy.

snippet: SC 4 instructs its Secretariat to submit the revised version of ISO 8000-1 “Data Quality - Overview, principles and general requirements”, excluding the taxonomy, to ISO/CS for TS publication by 20...

title: Manage ISO 15926 Reference Data Library as a database
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Rotterdam, Netherlands, November 8, 2009 -- November 13, 2009

actions.message: SC 4 resolves to manage the ISO 15926 Reference Data Library (RDL) using the ISO Annex ST procedures for the management of standards as databases, as customized in the SC 4 standing document “Procedure for the maintenance of ISO/TC 184/SC 4 reference data”.
SC 4 requests the WG 3/T 25 ISO 15926 part 3 and 4 projects to nominate one member each to the maintenance team.
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to invite P-members, Category A liaisons, internal liaisons, and the WG 3 convener to nominate additional experts, as needed under the agreed procedure.
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to invite all P-members, Category A liaisons and internal liaisons to appoint one member each to the validation team.

SC 4 requests its Secretariat to:

* Forward to ISO for publication an initial version of the RDL consisting of all ISO 15926 reference data that have already been published formally or have been successfully balloted as ready for publication (ISO/TS 15926-3, -4 and am1).
* Request that ISO publish the RDL on standards.iso.org under the same access conditions as the existing electronic inserts on the SC 4 site.
* Ask ISO to add references in the ISO Catalogue entries for ISO 15926 parts 3 and 4 referring to the RDL.
SC 4 approves a minor revision to create new editions of ISO 15926 parts 3 and 4 that point to the reference data online, rather than including the reference data as electronic attachments.

snippet: SC 4 resolves to manage the ISO 15926 Reference Data Library (RDL) using the ISO Annex ST procedures for the management of standards as databases, as customized in the SC 4 standing document “Proce...

SC 4 instructs the SC 4 secretariat to update the SC 4 standing document “Procedures for the maintenance of ISO/TS 10303-1000” correspondingly and to add that changes to ISO 10303 integrated resources and ISO 13584-20 shall pass a WG 12 integration review before a STEP Module Library change request process is initiated.

SC 4 will review progress on this part during its next meeting in May 2010.

title: Create ISO 29002-2 as vocabulary part for ISO 29002 series
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Rotterdam, Netherlands, November 8, 2009 -- November 13, 2009

actions.message: SC 4 decides to create 29002-2 as a vocabulary part for the entire 29002 series through merger of vocabulary from the TS versions of ISO 29002 parts 4, 5, 6, 10, 20, and 31.
SC 4 directs the project team to provide the Secretariat with a draft for DIS balloting as soon as possible, and directs the Secretariat to submit this part to ISO/CS for DIS ballot.
SC 4 decides that the normative version of all vocabulary used by the ISO 29002 series shall be in part 2, and replicated where the terms are used in individual parts.

snippet: SC 4 decides to create 29002-2 as a vocabulary part for the entire 29002 series through merger of vocabulary from the TS versions of ISO 29002 parts 4, 5, 6, 10, 20, and 31.

title: Approve PWI on Future SC 4 Architecture
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Parksville, Canada, May 17, 2009 -- May 22, 2009

actions.message:
SC 4 approves a PWI on “Future SC 4 Architecture” to:

* Explore the range of options for exploiting new technologies to support interoperability between SC 4 standards, and the work of other committees, operating across a range of platforms
* Recommend an overall architecture for future use by SC 4 standards
* Define migration paths for existing SC 4 capabilities
* Identify specific licensing, commercial or other administrative barriers
* Illustrate and validate any conclusions against a representative example
SC 4 invites all of its member bodies and WGs to contribute to the work of the PWI.
SC 4 appoints David Leal and David Price as joint leaders for the PWI and invites them to report back no later than the November 2009 meeting.

snippet: SC 4 approves a PWI on “Future SC 4 Architecture” to: * Explore the range of options for exploiting new technologies to support interoperability between SC 4 standards, and the work of other commit...

title: Create Advisory Group for SC 4 Implementation Forum
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Parksville, Canada, May 17, 2009 -- May 22, 2009

actions.message:
SC 4 decides to create the Advisory Group “SC 4 Implementation Forum”, which will also incorporate the tasks and members of the existing Committee for Education and Outreach, which is now disbanded. The group has the following tasks:

* Gather feed back from implementers
* Gather success stories
* Promote business benefits
* Promote awareness of capabilities
* Facilitate implementations
** Test model/Testing procedure
* Seek resources for sustainment
* Provide education and outreach

SC 4 requests its secretariat to invite participation from its member bodies and liaisons to undertake this work. Member bodies are invited to ensure the participation of STEP Centres and other supporting organizations.

Paul van Exel is nominated as leader of this group.
Anna Moreno is nominated as Co-deputy leader of this group.
Burton Gischner is nominated as Co-deputy leader of this group.

The Advisory Group is invited to present its work program to the November 2009 SC 4 Plenary meeting in November.

snippet: SC 4 decides to create the Advisory Group “SC 4 Implementation Forum”, which will also incorporate the tasks and members of the existing Committee for Education and Outreach, which is now disbanded...

title: Manage ISO 10303 application modules under part 1000 and adopt N2402
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Busan, Korea, October 27, 2008 -- November 7, 2008

actions.message: SC 4 resolves to manage the ISO 10303 application modules in the 400 and 1000 series using the ISO Procedures for the Management of standards as databases.
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to submit the operating procedures described in SC 4 N2401 for a one-month committee internal Yes/No ballot. Any negative votes must be accompanied by comments.

SC 4 requests its Secretariat to:

* Forward to ISO for publication an initial version of ISO/TS 10303-1000 consisting of all ISO 10303 application modules that have already been published or are ready for publication, with references to resource parts and AICs.
* Invite ISO to withdraw ISO 10303 parts 401 to 4xx and 1001 through 1xxx, marking each as having been replaced by part 1000, once Part 1000 is published.
* Propose that ISO modify its catalogue search function so that a search for any ISO 10303 part in the range 401 to 499 or 1001 to 1999 will return a message referring the user to part 1000.
* Propose to ISO that the publication strategy for ISO 10303 should offer a range of products, including:
** part 1000 as defined;
** individual APs and either the 1000 part or just the referenced application modules;
** CD with all ISO 10303 parts
SC 4 decides that no new ISO 10303 application modules or new editions of existing ISO 10303 application modules will be submitted for publication as separate parts, once Part 1000 is published.
SC 4 adopts the plan of action in SC 4 N2402.

snippet: SC 4 resolves to manage the ISO 10303 application modules in the 400 and 1000 series using the ISO Procedures for the Management of standards as databases.

title: Express concerns regarding ISO/TC 37/SC 3 NWI 22274
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Sun City, South Africa, June 8, 2008 -- June 13, 2008

actions.message:
SC4 expresses the following concerns regarding NWI 22274 from ISO/TC 37/SC 3:

* This proposal appears to violate the agreements made at the ISO/TC 37 Beijing 2006 and Provo 2007 meetings.
* SC4's request that ISO/TC 37/SC 3 delay circulating a NWI proposal for 22274 until a harmonised position between ISO/TC 184/SC 4 and ISO/TC 37 could be agreed and until a draft could be produced that could be circulated as a combined NWI/CD was not followed.
* This proposal seems to have expanded to cover product classification schemes, which are within scope of SC4 and not of ISO/TC 37.
SC4 directs the Chair, Secretariat, and liaison officers to ISO/TC 37/SC 3 to jointly develop a liaison statement expressing SC4's concerns about this project, for submission to ISO/TC 37/SC 3.
SC4 requests the liaison officers to coordinate an SC4 review of the NWI and prepare a set of ballot comments, noting the ballot closing date of 2008-07-31.
SC4 encourages its member countries to submit corresponding ballot comments through their national bodies.

title: Address ISO 14649 patent issues to avoid restricting standards
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Dallas, United States, October 28, 2007 -- November 2, 2007

actions.message: SC4 expresses its extreme concern at the violation of the common ITU-R/ITU-T/ISO/IEC patent policy by the issuance of patents on the implementation of ISO 14649, and the resulting proposal for further similar patents being registered to protect implementation of SC4 standards. All committees within ISO, IEC and ITU may be placed into a similar situation by granting of patents that restrict implementation of ISO standards. Therefore SC4 requests ISO/CS to undertake the necessary actions to avoid this problem in the future, and invites its P-members to pursue the matter through their national bodies.

snippet: SC4 expresses its extreme concern at the violation of the common ITU-R/ITU-T/ISO/IEC patent policy by the issuance of patents on the implementation of ISO 14649, and the resulting proposal for furt...

title: Create a Big Picture showing relationship of standards
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Dallas, United States, October 28, 2007 -- November 2, 2007

actions.message:
SC4 requests the PPC to continue and extend its existing work to generate a "Big Picture" illustrating the relationship and interaction of standards created by ISO/TC 184 in an operational environment, and seeks participation from other ISO/TC 184 Subcommittees in this work. The PPC may also invite additional SC4 experts to participate in this work.

The target groups for the results will be:

* SC4 experts - to reach a common understanding
* Standardizers of other committees - to show how ISO/TC 184 standards can be used within their standards activities
* Industry - to understand which standards might be used to solve their problems in areas such as collaboration, interoperability, data exchange
SC4 requests its Chair and Secretariat to encourage the other SCs of ISO/TC 184 to participate in this work.

SC4 requests the group to take full account of the results of previous initiatives in this area. A presentation of results is expected for the meeting of ISO/TC 184 in Oct. 2008.

Action item: A SharePoint service will be installed by the SC4 Secretariat to facilitate cooperation on this topic.

title: Amends resolution 674 regarding cooperation with ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 31 on AIDC
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Toulouse, France, June 30, 2006

actions.message:
SC 4 replaces the first paragraph of resolution 674 with the following:

"SC 4 expresses its interest in taking responsibility for Industrial Data application standards for all automatic identification data capture (AIDC) technologies (not limited to radio frequency identification [RFID]), in cooperation with ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 31."

SC 4 agrees to extend the ad hoc group until SC 31 and ISO/TC 184 and its SC 4 define and agree the scope of the work required within their respective committees, and jointly prepare and agree any necessary New Work Item Proposals for consideration by national bodies.

SC 4 requests its delegation to provide a written report within two weeks of each physical meeting, and an oral report at each SC 4 meeting.

Note: Resolution amends RESOLUTION 674 (Vico Equense, Italy - March 2006).

considerations.message: SC 4 recognizes that formal liaison arrangements with SC 31 may need to be implemented at some future date.

actions.message: SC 4 approves the nomination of Prof. Guy Pierra to serve for an additional three year term.

SC 4 requires that SC 4 modules normatively referenced by modular STEP Application Protocols (APs) shall complete their Committee Draft Technical Specification (CD-TS) ballot prior to the AP completing its Draft International Standard (DIS) ballot. title: Establish Working Group 13 on Industrial Data Quality
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Vico Equense, Italy, March 5, 2006 -- March 10, 2006

actions.message:
SC 4 resolves to establish a new Working Group 13, Industrial Data Quality.

The task of this working group will be to:

* Establish a coherent definition of the structure of Quality Standards to be produced by SC 4, based on the results of the Product Data Quality Strategy meeting held on 2006-03-07 (SC 4 N2078)
* Identify NWI and develop the necessary standards for accreditation of organizations and processes, and the quality criteria for information complying with each of the SC 4 standards, all to be published under the ISO 8000 series.
* The current ISO 8000 work item will become part of the ISO 8000 series.
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to invite National Member Bodies to nominate experts and candidates for Convenorship for the group.

title: Adopt policy for format and layout of SC 4 standards
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Valencia, Spain, June 17, 2005

actions.message:
SC 4 adopts the following policy with regard to format and layout of SC 4 standards:

* Parts delivered to ISO in HTML shall follow the format and layout as given in
the HTML Guidelines.
* Parts delivered to ISO in PDF shall follow the appropriate format and layout
as given in the current clause 5, Format and layout of SC 4 standards, of the
Supplementary Directives.
* Parts delivered to ISO in Microsoft® Word shall be prepared with one of the
ISO templates for drafting standards.

This policy shall go into effect immediately.

SC 4 directs the Quality Committee to make the appropriate changes to the
Supplementary Directives to implement the above policy.

title: Request wording in standards regarding translated versions
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Lillehammer, Norway, February 27, 2005 -- March 4, 2005

actions.message:
SC 4 requests the NSBs to review their policies for translating standards which might
be a basis for software applications, in order to avoid creating incompatibilities
between implementations.

The following wording shall be incorporated in any introduction of the SC 4
standards:

* "Attention: This standard provides a specification which can be used for
the development of software. Any implementation of software based on a
translation of this standard into national languages other than the
official languages of ISO may cause incompatibility in machine-machine
communication. In order to avoid such incompatibilities, the agreed
international standard is the governing version."

SC 4 requests its Quality Committee to insert this language into the Supplementary
Directives for incorporation in all future publications.

title: Create ad hoc group for SC 4 Maintenance Agency procedures
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Lillehammer, Norway, February 27, 2005 -- March 4, 2005

actions.message:
SC 4 creates an ad hoc group with the task of developing a unified approach to
specify the procedures for a Maintenance Agency administered by the SC 4
secretariat to support reference data databases for SC 4 standards available on the web.

This group will create a proposal for the Maintenance Agency to be submitted to the
ISO Technical Management Board (TMB) and will develop a business case to present
to the ISO Council as to why these reference data libraries can be accessed free of
charge from an appropriately serviced site.

Chris Kreiler is appointed as the Chair of this ad hoc group. Other members should be
nominated by PPC, WG 2, WG 3, and other interested SC 4 committees nominated by
end of March 2005. SC 4 request the group report back to the SC 4 meeting in Valencia.

title: Request PPC justify free web availability of selected SC 4 standards
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Lillehammer, Norway, February 27, 2005 -- March 4, 2005

actions.message:
SC 4 believes that the free of charge availability of selected SC 4 standards will further
promote the adoption of those standards and increase their visibility in a highly
competitive marketplace particularly for small and medium enterprises.

SC 4 would like to follow the path of ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 32 in which certain of their
standards, including ISO 11179, are freely available on the Web, and revenue is
generated through tutorials and implementer sessions.

SC 4 requests the PPC to examine new business models for generating alternative
sources of revenue based on SC 4 standards.

SC 4 requests the PPC to prepare a justification to the ISO Council for approving that
ISO 10303 Application Protocol 203 and its normative references within ISO 10303
should be made freely available on the Web.

considerations.message:
Based on recent investigations within US and UK standards organizations, SC 4 notes
that very little revenue is being generated from the sale of SC 4 standards despite
very significant potential for industrial benefit from their use.

snippet: SC 4 believes that the free of charge availability of selected SC 4 standards will further promote the adoption of those standards and increase their visibility in a highly competitive marketplace ...

title: Split ISO 13584-501 and direct IEC SC 65B liaison actions
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Seattle, United States, October 3, 2004 -- October 8, 2004

actions.message:
SC 4 decides to split the scope of the registered dictionary defined by ISO 13584 Part
501 (ISO id 39753) into two parts, covering (a) laboratory and environment measuring
instruments and (b) industrial process measuring instruments.

SC 4 directs WG 2 to stop the work on item (b) and directs its Secretariat to offer the
work on this item to IEC SC 65B for potential publication either as a joint ISO/IEC
standard or as an IEC standard.

SC 4 directs WG 2 to continue the work on item (a), and authorizes a new delivery
date for the DIS ballot of 2004-11-30.

SC 4 invites IEC experts to contribute to the work on item (a) in WG 2, both during
the further development of the standard and during the operation of the registration
authority.

SC 4 encourages WG 2 experts who are also members of IEC to
participate in the work of IEC SC 65B on item (b).

SC 4 instructs WG 2 to take into account forthcoming results of ISO/IEC JWG 1
concerning the maintenance of dictionaries during the preparation of the next
versions of the ISO 13584-501 standard.

title: Create NWI for lifecycle support of AEC facilities
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Seattle, United States, October 3, 2004 -- October 8, 2004

actions.message:
The goal of this PWI is to create a NWI for a model for lifecycle support of AEC
facilities. The following tasks will be performed:

* Detailed in scope, out of scope analysis of the information requirements of
AEC facilities including information requirement for facility management.
* Review of capability of existing SC 4 standards to cover lifecycle aspects such
as facility management. Describe to which extent they cover the information
requirements of AEC facilities
* Describe gaps and necessary adaptations
* Explore feasibility and best approach to combine existing standards. The
different approaches of developing a modular AP or a conventional approach
will be assessed.

SC 4 directs WG 3 to create the NWI within 6 months forward it to SC 4 for review and
approval, and appoints Inhan Kim and Wolfgang Haas as co project leaders.

Attached documents: Description of the PWI, ISO/TC 184/SC 4/WG 3 N1505, change
management project review questionnaire, ISO/TC 184/SC 4/WG 3 N1506.

snippet: The goal of this PWI is to create a NWI for a model for lifecycle support of AEC facilities. The following tasks will be performed: * Detailed in scope, out of scope analysis of the information req...

title: Request ISO CS consider module progress to avoid AP cancellation
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Seattle, United States, October 3, 2004 -- October 8, 2004

actions.message:
SC 4 requests ISO to consider the progress of individual modules when
deciding whether it is appropriate to cancel an AP Project for lack of progress.
In particular, ballot submission of a substantive set of the new implementation and
foundation modules that are included within the scope of an AP (as defined in its
NWI), should also be considered as a change in the status for the overall AP
document, and should therefore preclude its cancellation for lack of progress.

SC 4 requests its Secretariat to ask ISO to apply this approach to the development of
AP233 as a test case, noting the substantive progress on an initial set of modules, and
the threat of cancellation of the AP project in January 2005.

title: Establish task force for dictionaries and libraries white paper
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Poitiers, France, October 25, 2003 -- October 31, 2003

actions.message:
SC 4 establishes a task force to produce a white paper on the scope, relationship between ISO 10303, ISO 13584 and ISO 15926 and their industrial usage to meet business requirements for the definition of dictionaries, part libraries and reference data libraries. The white paper will be issued by the 15th of February 2004 and the team will report to SC 4 at the next ISO/TC 184 SC 4 meeting in Ft Lauderdale, spring 2004.

The results shall be clearly understandable by SC 4 experts and stakeholders, in order to promote consistent presentation of SC 4 products to the market place.

ISO/TC 184/SC 4 appoints the following task force team members:

* Gerry Radack (Task force team leader)
* Guy Pierra

* Hiroshi Murayama
* Margaret Christison
* Ricardo Gonçalves
* Nils Sandsmark

* Paul van Exel
* Norman Swindells
* Yamine AIT-AMEUR
* Junfeng Zhan

SC 4 endorses the response of its Chair (Document SC 4 N1606) which notes that the ISO Council has not addressed several of the issues raised in the previous SC 4 Resolution.

SC 4 further urges its member national bodies to approach their ISO Council and TMB members and impress upon them the urgency and importance of this issue.

Attached documents: SC 4 N1606, SC 4 N1456

title: Inform ISO/TC 10 of impact of ISO 16792 and encourage consensus
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Poitiers, France, October 25, 2003 -- October 31, 2003

actions.message: SC 4 requests its secretariat to inform ISO/TC 10 that the planned publication of ISO 16792 will lead to significant changes in already existing and published standards by SC 4.

SC 4 encourages ISO/TC 10 and ISO/TC 213 to reach a clear consensus on unambiguous and consistent representations of 2D and 3D annotation that can be referenced by the SC 4 standards for digital representation of product information when they are revised, since it will not be viable to support conflicting forms of annotation.
Further, SC 4 asks ISO/TC 10 to provide a clear strategic statement on their future development plans; especially because SC 4 has referenced several ISO/TC 10 standards on annotation and drafting.

Action: The teams of AP 202, 203, 210, 212, 214 and are requested to check the potential impact of the content of ISO16792 against their projects, with a view to directly referencing the standard in future.

snippet: SC 4 requests its secretariat to inform ISO/TC 10 that the planned publication of ISO 16792 will lead to significant changes in already existing and published standards by SC 4.

title: Submit liaison statement to ISO/TC 59 regarding building construction modeling
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Poitiers, France, October 25, 2003 -- October 31, 2003

actions.message: SC 4 requests its secretariat to submit a liaison statement to ISO/TC 59 "Building construction" explaining the area of work of SC 4, especially to point out the activities of its WG 2 and WG 3 work on modelling within the area of building construction.

SC 4 also requests ISO/TC 59, especially ISO/TC 59/SC 13/WG 6, to identify areas of need for harmonization with SC 4 standards such as ISO 10303-225 and ISO 15926 in its work programme, in order to foster cooperation and collaboration in the future.
SC 4 appoints Tatsuo Terai as a physical liaison to ISO/TC 59/SC 13/WG 6 in order to support the required cooperation and requests its Secretariat to establish the liaison.
Action: National Bodies of SC 4 should examine this initiative to promote ISO 12006 as a DIS and assess it against the existing procurement regulations in their countries.

snippet: SC 4 requests its secretariat to submit a liaison statement to ISO/TC 59 "Building construction" explaining the area of work of SC 4, especially to point out the activities of its WG 2 and WG 3 wor...

title: Request ISO/CS to confirm adjustability of development milestones
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Poitiers, France, October 25, 2003 -- October 31, 2003

actions.message: SC 4 therefore requests ISO/CS to confirm that the intermediate milestones of the new timeframes can be adjusted to allow appropriate work planning, within the overall time limits given.
SC 4 also seeks confirmation that ISO/CS can achieve its timescales for processing, noting that there are examples of FDIS processing exceeding three months.

considerations.message: SC 4 notes the objectives of the new timeframes and accepts the overall timescales proposed, although it must be recognised that reduced timescales are often incompatible with adequate quality, consensus and market relevance. However, SC 4 notes that the intermediate milestones proposed in the new timeframe do not take sufficient account of the need for technical discussion and consensus building, while allowing extended periods for publication and administration. The use of publication tools to accelerate the administrative tasks means that SC 4 does not need to exploit the full time allowed for administration, but requires the maximum time possible for technical work.

snippet: SC 4 therefore requests ISO/CS to confirm that the intermediate milestones of the new timeframes can be adjusted to allow appropriate work planning, within the overall time limits given.

actions.message: To avoid duplication or contradictory work, SC 4 wishes to encourage ISO/TC 37 to inform ISO/TC 184/SC 4/WG 2 about the actual status on "Basic principles of multilingual classification for electronic commerce" and related activities.

Required actions following approval of this resolution: To submit this resolution directly to ISO/TC 37 secretariat with a request to provide information about their results and plans and to contact ISO/TC 184/SC 4/WG 2 with the goal to establish a liaison to identify overlapping or contradicting work (Secretariat).

title: Extend Task Force for Industrial Data on the Web
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, San Diego, United States, March 10, 2003 -- March 14, 2003

actions.message:
SC 4 directs the ad hoc group for Industrial Data on the Web (IDW) to report regularly
to SC 4 plenaries concerning the impact of established and emerging Web
technologies on the work of SC 4. These technologies include web services, such as
WSDL, UDDI and LDAP.

IDW will be a mechanism by which web-related technologies developed by individual
SC 4 WGs are shared.

IDW will deliver SC 4 resolutions, tutorials, N docs and technology demonstrations.

SC 4 urges the ad hoc group to recommend liaisons with W3C committees, and to
coordinate their activities with the Education and Outreach Committee.

SC 4 appoints Jim U'Ren as the leader of this ad hoc group, and David Leal as the
deputy.

IDW will deliver a report to the last SC 4 meeting in 2004 with a recommendation as to
the continuation of the ad hoc group.

title: Cancel Resolution 462 and integrate module harmonization
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, San Diego, United States, March 10, 2003 -- March 14, 2003

actions.message: SC 4 cancels its Resolution 462 that established a STEP Modules Harmonization Team.

SC 4 urges its Working Groups 3, 8, 9 and 12 along with the active module development
projects to cooperate in module harmonization.

SC 4 requests the conveners of WG 3, 8, 9, and 12 to report on module
harmonization activities to plenary meetings of SC 4 and its Working Groups.

SC 4 also urges its Working Groups 3, 8, 9 and 12 to complete and update the
guidelines documents for module creation.

In particular, SC 4 requests that guidance on module harmonization be added to the
Module Development Guidelines, and that consideration be given to the use of ISO
18876 in this context.

snippet: SC 4 cancels its Resolution 462 that established a STEP Modules Harmonization Team.

title: Streamline publication of Modular APs
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Seoul, Korea, November 3, 2002 -- November 8, 2002

actions.message:
SC 4 requires all projects developing modular APs and Application Modules to use
the XSL development environment in the STEP Repository. Deviations from this
requirement shall be approved by the appropriate Convenors and the Quality
Committee.

SC 4 requests its secretariat to register new AP modules as 10303 parts in the 400
series. The AP module number for an AP 2xy shall be 4xy.

SC 4 permits modular APs to be submitted directly to DIS ballot following successful
completion of the TS ballots for their component modules.

SC 4 requests its secretariat to negotiate with ISO for the publication of standard
products comprising APs and all their supporting modules.

SC 4 requests its member bodies and the affected projects to nominate resources to an
ad hoc team to develop revised AP Guidelines and Supplementary Directives.

considerations.message:
Noting the significant progress made in the definition of the structure and content
of modular APs, which shall be published as an AP with a complementary AP
module.

title: Assert SC 4 position on overlaps with ISO/IEC 62264
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Seoul, Korea, November 3, 2002 -- November 8, 2002

actions.message:
SC 4 asks its member bodies to take appropriate actions to ensure that these comments
will be taken into account in their answer to the ballot, from the ISO side as well as
from the IEC side.

SC 4 also asks ISO/TC 184 to clarify as far as possible the situation with IEC/TC 65
and to improve the collaboration already set up by the SC 5/JWG 15 to include SC 4
participation to that working group.

Moreover SC 4 asks ISO/TC 184 to take appropriate actions to ensure that ISO and
IEC committees with scopes that may overlap or relate to its own subcommittees are
aware of the standards already published and the work in progress in ISO/TC 184.

considerations.message:
Noting that ISO/TC 184/SC 4 notices some overlaps and inconsistencies between ISO/IEC 62264
and some of its existing developed standards or work in progress. Although some
normative references have been added in part 1 in the framework of ballot comment
resolution, SC 4 is concerned that insufficient consideration of the SC 4 approach,
work and constructs have been made in ISO/IEC 62264-2 which is currently under
ballot through a fast-track procedure.

This especially concerns the objects product, material, equipment, resource,
personnel, product segment, personnel segment, capability segment in ISO/IEC
62264-2 versus product, resource, capability, event, time as they are defined in
STEP, MANDATE and other SC 4 standards. In addition, some of the objects as they
are defined in ISO/IEC 62264-2 are not independent one from the other and contains
their relationship to the other. Time is not managed independently and appears in
each object. That is also incompatible with the SC 4 approach.

Since ISO/TC 184 standards and ISO/IEC 62264 address strongly related and
overlapping area of work, this situation may lead to conflict, difficulties and to the
unnecessary duplication of work.

snippet: SC 4 asks its member bodies to take appropriate actions to ensure that these comments will be taken into account in their answer to the ballot, from the ISO side as well as from the IEC side.

title: Transpose the IAI IFC2x Specification as an ISO PAS
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Stockholm, Sweden, June 9, 2002 -- June 15, 2002

actions.message:
SC 4 invites the IAI to submit the IFC2x Platform Specification, along with the final
version of the explanatory report, to the Secretariat for letter ballot for publication as an
SC 4 PAS, under the SC 4 Harvesting procedure.

SC 4 requests that a joint activity be established between its working groups 3/11/12
and the IAI to harmonize the IFC2x Platform Specification and existing SC 4
standards, prior to the 3 year mandatory review of the PAS.

SC 4 proposes that its activity be focused within ISO/TC 184/SC 4 WG 3/T22, and
invites the Convenors of WG 3/11/12 to nominate appropriate representatives to
support this work.

subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Stockholm, Sweden, June 9, 2002 -- June 15, 2002

actions.message: SC 4 strongly supports the objectives of the task force created by ISO/TC 184/AG.

SC 4 member bodies and liaisons are encouraged to provide their views relative to the
scope of the ISO/TC 184 Business Strategy and Development task force, especially in
terms of:

* relevance of the current SC 4 work items to their industrial needs,
* fields of industrial data not yet covered,
* priorities and available expertise.

SC 4 strongly objects to any subdivision of SC 4 without an adequate business case
subject to approval by the member bodies.

Input should be forwarded to SC 4 Chair and Secretariat, no later than end of
September 2002.

title: Establish comment resolution procedure for Technical Specifications
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Myrtle Beach, United States, February 25, 2002 -- March 1, 2002

actions.message:
SC 4 agrees that the following procedure shall be applied to the resolution of
comments submitted against successful Technical Specification ballots.

1) Resolution of comments shall be agreed with the originators, where possible.

2) The revised document incorporating the agreed comment resolutions shall be
circulated within the development team and to the commentors for internal review to
ensure agreement before signoff by the Convenor.

3) If the Convenor, in conjunction with the Project Leader, considers that the revised
document contains significant change, other than corrections to the use of English,
the document shall be passed to the Secretariat for a confirmatory four-week ballot.

This procedure is effective immediately, and SC 4 requests the Change Management
Committee to prepare an advance resolution for the June 2002 meeting to make the
necessary changes to the SC 4 Handbook.

subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Fukuoka, Japan, September 30, 2001 -- October 5, 2001

actions.message:
SC 4 cancels Resolution 436 and establishes the following policy with regard to
publication of standards in HTML.

SC 4 resolves to allow SC 4 projects to enter any stage ballot with drafts documented
using HTML. It is the responsibility of the Project Leader to ensure that any
documents entering Stage 4 or higher are in a form acceptable to the ISO Central
Secretariat. The provisions of Resolution 395 with respect to the use of guideline
development by the WG 10 STEP Modularization PWI remain in effect.

SC 4 instructs the Quality Committee to develop guidelines for the use of HTML for
publication of documents, with reference to guidance provided by ISO CS and the
ITSIG "Guide for the use of IT in the development and delivery of standards".

title: Direct WG 10 to assess Application Modules referencing IS Application Protocols
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Fukuoka, Japan, September 30, 2001 -- October 5, 2001

actions.message:
SC 4 directs WG 10, in cooperation with WG 3, JWG 9, WG 11, WG 12, and the
Implementors Forum, to assess the feasibility of allowing Application Modules to
accurately and unambiguously reference content from IS Application Protocols rather
than re-working the AP content, to identify and resolve any issues arising from this
process, and to identify the changes required to the following documents:

* Guidelines for the contents of Application Modules
* Application Module development points within AP development process
* Guidelines for the contents of APs that use Application Modules

The work should address issues related to configuration management and the context,
constraints, and rules associated with reference elements.

SC 4 invites its member bodies to nominate appropriate resources to WG 10 to
accomplish the task. WG 10 is invited to report on this issue at the February 2002
meeting.

source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Fukuoka, Japan, September 30, 2001 -- October 5, 2001

actions.message:
SC 4 expresses its deep appreciation to Mr. Julian Fowler for his untiring efforts as
Deputy Coordinator of the Quality Committee. It is with great regret that SC 4 accepts
his resignation and his announcement of reduced contributions to the SC 4 world.
Julian has been a major pillar to the organization since its earliest days, and his
contributions in developing consensus and convergence has always been valued. His
willingness to take on unpopular, but essential tasks, has been exemplary, and the
challenge of finding a replacement will not be trivial.

SC 4 wishes him well in his new role!

title: Create ad hoc group on Industrial data on the web
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, San Francisco, United States, June 10, 2001 -- June 15, 2001

actions.message:
SC 4 creates an ad hoc group to investigate how SC 4's standards can be used for
communication of industrial data using the internet and the World Wide Web. The
primary focus of the ad hoc group shall be the use of standards developed by SC 4
as specifications of the content of transactions, messages, product data sets, and
other aspects of internet and intranet based communications.

This group shall deliver to SC 4 recommendations that identify the actions necessary
to exploit the applications of SC 4's standards to the internet and to align SC 4's
standards with those used for internet communications. These recommendations may
include (but are not limited to) the following:

* establishment of formal liaisons with industrial initiatives, consortia,
  professional organisations, or standards bodies;
* revisions to existing SC 4 standards;
* initiation of new SC 4 projects (preliminary work items or new work items);
* marketing initiatives that promote the applicability of SC 4's standards to
  the internet

SC 4 directs the ad hoc group to liaise with the team established by WG 3 and the
QC to create guidelines for the use of XML and HTML for development and
publication of SC 4 standards. The ad hoc group should also take into consideration
the results of the PWI on SGML and Industrial Data (WG 10 n324 Annex B).

SC 4 directs the ad hoc group to present its initial results to the SC 4 meeting in
Fukuoka (October 2001) and its final results to the SC 4 meeting in Myrtle Beach SC
(March 2002).
SC 4 appoints Julian Fowler as the leader of this ad hoc group.

SC 4 requests the conveners of WG 2, WG 3, JWG 8, JWG 9, WG 10, WG 11, and WG 12, and the
coordinator of the Quality Committee to each nominate at least one expert to the ad
hoc group.

SC 4 requests its P-members and A-liaisons to nominate additional
experts to the ad hoc group by the end of July.

The group will be disbanded in March 2002.

title: Endorse publication of Part 28 as a Technical Specification
subject: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
source_title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Funchal, Portugal, February 19, 2001 -- February 23, 2001

actions.message:
SC 4 endorses the following approach to publication of Part 28 as a Technical
Specification:

* the late binding and both early bindings will be kept in this version of the
  Tech. Spec.
* provision for bi-directional inter-operability between the early bindings
  using XSLT will be added
* objective is to revise the document for publication by June meeting

Following publication of the initial Part 28, work on an XML Schema binding will begin
as requested in the ballot comments. The XML Schema solution will not be
constrained to be upward compatible with the DTD solutions.

SC 4 requests Japan, France and USA to re-consider their votes based on the above
actions to achieve consensus. In addition, member countries are requested to provide
resources to complete the ballot resolution and editing process.
